_info: Rich English historical context for HUD v3 overlay. Hand-crafted from German historical_note data.

459.title: Pre-War Era
459.context: Final peacetime newsreel before the outbreak of war. Europe teeters on the brink as diplomatic tensions reach breaking point.

468.title: Nazi-Soviet Pact
468.context: Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact signed August 23, 1939. Secret protocol divides Eastern Europe between Germany and the Soviet Union.

470.title: War Begins
470.context: FIRST WARTIME NEWSREEL EVER PRODUCED. Germany invades Poland on September 1, 1939. World War II begins.

471.title: Poland Campaign
471.context: Blitzkrieg — a new form of lightning warfare sweeps through Poland. Mechanized divisions stun the world with their speed.

473.title: Fall of Warsaw
473.context: Warsaw surrenders after weeks of relentless siege and bombardment. September 27, 1939 — Poland's capital falls.

477.title: Poland Occupied
477.context: The General Government is established over occupied Poland. Millions placed under German military administration.

480.title: Bürgerbräu Bomb
480.context: Georg Elser's lone assassination attempt on Hitler. November 8, 1939 — bomb detonates 13 minutes after Hitler leaves the beer hall.

482.title: Winter War Begins
482.context: Soviet Union invades Finland on November 30, 1939. The Winter War begins — tiny Finland faces the Red Army alone.

483.title: Finnish Resistance
483.context: Outnumbered Finnish forces hold their ground against the Red Army. The world watches in awe as Finland fights back.

488.title: Phoney War
488.context: The Western Front remains eerily quiet. Troops face each other across the Maginot Line while nations prepare for total war.

502.title: Norway Invasion
502.context: Operation Weserübung — Germany invades Denmark and Norway simultaneously. April 9, 1940 — Scandinavia drawn into the war.

508.title: Dunkirk Pocket
508.context: 400,000 Allied soldiers trapped at Dunkirk. May 26 to June 4, 1940 — the greatest military evacuation in history begins.

509.title: Dunkirk Evacuation
509.context: Operation Dynamo. 340,000 Allied soldiers evacuated across the English Channel by a flotilla of warships and civilian boats.

512.title: French Armistice
512.context: Armistice signed at Compiègne, June 22, 1940. France falls in just six weeks. Hitler's revenge for the 1918 surrender.

513.title: Channel Islands
513.context: British Channel Islands occupied — the only British home territory under German control for the entire war.

514.title: Berlin Victory Parade
514.context: Wehrmacht victory parade through Berlin. The German capital celebrates the fall of France with military pageantry.

515.title: Battle of Britain
515.context: The Battle of Britain begins July 10, 1940. Luftwaffe vs. RAF - the first major all-air military campaign in history.

518.title: Channel Battles
518.context: Aerial dogfights rage over the English Channel. Luftwaffe convoy attacks intensify as the air war escalates.

519.title: Eagle Day
519.context: Adlertag — Eagle Day, August 13, 1940. Massive Luftwaffe assault targets RAF airfields and radar stations across southern England.

520.title: Battle at its Peak
520.context: The Battle of Britain reaches its climax. RAF Fighter Command is stretched to breaking point — the fate of Britain hangs in the balance.

523.title: London Blitz
523.context: The Blitz begins September 7, 1940. Luftwaffe shifts from attacking airfields to bombing London — 57 consecutive nights of terror.

542.title: Tobruk Falls
542.context: British forces capture Tobruk on January 22, 1941. Italian garrison surrenders — the desert war shifts momentum.

543.title: Afrika Korps
543.context: Erwin Rommel dispatched to North Africa. The legendary Afrika Korps is formed to rescue Italy's crumbling desert campaign.

544.title: Rommel Arrives
544.context: Rommel lands in Tripoli, February 12, 1941. The Desert Fox begins his legendary campaign across the Sahara.

545.title: Africa Offensive
545.context: German counteroffensive begins in North Africa. Rommel pushes the British back across Libya with bold armored thrusts.

547.title: Africa Campaign
547.context: Rommel drives British forces eastward. The Afrika Korps recaptures Cyrenaica in a stunning desert reversal.

548.title: Bulgaria Joins Axis
548.context: Bulgaria joins the Axis powers on March 1, 1941. Wehrmacht troops enter Bulgarian territory — the Balkans become the next front.

550.title: Yugoslavia Crisis
550.context: Yugoslavia joins the Axis on March 25, 1941. A military coup follows two days later — setting the stage for German invasion.

573.title: Battle of Kiev
573.context: Largest encirclement battle in military history. 665,000 Soviet soldiers captured — an entire army group destroyed at Kiev.

751.title: Eastern Front Collapses
751.context: The Eastern Front disintegrates. Soviet forces advance relentlessly westward — nothing can stop the Red Army's march toward Berlin.

753.title: Yalta Conference
753.context: Yalta Conference, February 4-11, 1945. Roosevelt, Churchill and Stalin meet to divide post-war Europe among the victors.

754.title: Dresden Bombed
754.context: Allied firebombing of Dresden, February 13-15, 1945. One of the most devastating aerial bombardments of the entire war.

522.title: Berlin Retaliation
522.context: RAF retaliates with bombing raids on Berlin, August 25-26, 1940. Hitler orders the Blitz on London in response — a fateful strategic mistake.

652.title: Third Battle of Kharkov
652.context: Third Battle of Kharkov, February-March 1943. Manstein's bold counteroffensive recaptures the city — last major German victory in the East.

654.title: Tunisia Battles
654.context: Fierce battles rage across Tunisia. Allied forces close in on the Afrika Korps from both east and west — the desert war nears its end.

720.title: V-1 Flying Bombs
720.context: V-1 flying bombs terrorize London from June 13, 1944. Hitler's 'Vergeltungswaffe' — vengeance weapon — strikes from across the Channel.

721.title: Bagration Begins
721.context: Operation Bagration launched June 22, 1944. The largest Soviet offensive of the war — three years to the day after Barbarossa began.

722.title: Army Group Center Destroyed
722.context: Army Group Center annihilated. 28 of 34 divisions destroyed — the greatest German military catastrophe of the war. 300,000 soldiers lost.

746.title: Battle of the Bulge
746.context: Ardennes Offensive — Hitler's last gamble. 250,000 German troops launch a surprise attack through the Belgian forests. December 16, 1944.

750.title: Vistula-Oder Offensive
750.context: Vistula-Oder Offensive begins January 12, 1945. 2.2 million Soviet soldiers smash through German lines — the road to Berlin is open.
